sections.paragraph:
μισέω [etym. obscure]

—1. ‘have strong dislike for’ some pers. or thing, hate Mt 5:43; 10:22; Mk 13:13; Lk 1:71; 6:27; 19:14; J 3:20; 7:7; 15:23; Ro 7:15; Eph 5:29; Tit 3:3; Hb 1:9; 1 J 2:9; Jd 23; Rv 2:6; 17:16; 18:2.

—2. ‘consider unworthy of notice or special interest’, disdain Mt 6:24; J 12:25. In Lk 14:26; Ro 9:13 an Israelite view of covenantal choice embeds itself in the use of μ., somewhat in the sense of our ‘have one take a back seat’; the antonym ἀγαπάω would then = ‘show preference to’.
